name: lrucache
description: A bounded, generic LRU cache (Cache[K comparable, V any]) with O(1) Get/Put built on container/list + a map, standard library only, go 1.23. The doubly linked list orders entries by recency (front = MRU, back = LRU) and the map gives constant-time key lookups; inserting beyond capacity evicts the least-recently-used entry. Exposes New (with capacity validation), Get (promotes), Peek/Contains (no promotion), Put (insert/update + eviction signal), Remove, Len, Cap, Keys (MRU->LRU snapshot), and Clear. Table-driven tests cover capacity validation, insert/update, eviction order, recency promotion vs. Peek non-promotion, removal freeing space, key ordering, Clear reuse, and repeated eviction at capacity 1. Verified gofmt-clean, build/vet/test all green.

maintain.request: Add an optional eviction callback so callers can observe entries that are dropped due to capacity overflow (e.g. to close handles, flush dirty values, or update metrics). Introduce a functional-options API: a new WithOnEvict option passed to New, invoked with the key and value of each entry evicted as least-recently-used. The callback must fire ONLY on capacity-driven eviction, not on explicit Remove, Clear, or in-place value updates, and a nil callback must be safely ignored. Keep New backward compatible (existing single-argument New calls must still compile and behave identically).

review.bug: The capacity-overflow check in Put uses `c.ll.Len() >= c.capacity` instead of `> c.capacity`, an off-by-one that evicts the least-recently-used entry one insertion too early so the cache only ever holds capacity-1 entries (and 0 when capacity is 1).
